claimText: The Stratospheric Airship Platform (SAP) was developed by the Aerial Delivery Research and Development Establishment (ADRDE) under DRDO.
evidenceExcerpt: SAP was developed by the Aerial Delivery Research and Development Establishment (ADRDE), Agra under DRDO.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the developing agency of SAP.

claimText: SAP operates in the stratosphere at an altitude of 17 to 22 kilometers above Earth.
evidenceExcerpt: It operates in the stratosphere, typically between 17 to 22 kilometers above Earth.
examUse: Statement-based question on the operational atmospheric layer and altitude of SAP.

claimText: SAP functions as a High-Altitude Pseudo-Satellite (HAPS) that utilizes helium for buoyancy and solar energy for power.
evidenceExcerpt: Functions as a High-Altitude Pseudo-Satellite (HAPS), using helium for buoyancy and solar energy with onboard batteries for power.
examUse: Statement-based question on the propulsion, buoyancy, and power mechanisms of HAPS.

claimText: High-Altitude Pseudo-Satellites (HAPS) bridge the operational gap between drones and satellites by providing space-like coverage with lower costs and faster deployment.
evidenceExcerpt: HAPS bridges the gap between drones and satellites, offering space-like coverage with faster deployment and lower costs.
examUse: Comparison question evaluating the advantages of HAPS over traditional satellites and drones.
distractorHooks: Limited to line-of-sight coverage like standard drones
